settings.text: Three retainers, sized by the depth of coverage. Pick the tier that matches the risk profile of your team — change tiers any quarter as you grow.

settings.title: Three ways to run your IT.
settings.description: Per-seat monthly pricing, billed on active headcount. No hourly billing for anything inside the scope.

settings.title: Essentials
settings.features:
+ Business-hours helpdesk
+ Endpoint monitoring & patching
+ Microsoft 365 or Google Workspace admin
+ Email & DNS security
+ Quarterly business review

settings.title: Professional
settings.features:
+ Everything in Essentials
+ After-hours emergency line
+ Managed firewall & Wi-Fi
+ Endpoint detection & response
+ Security awareness training
+ Backup for workstations & mailboxes

settings.title: Enterprise
settings.features:
+ Everything in Professional
+ 24/7 response & on-call engineer
+ vCIO strategy & roadmapping
+ Server & cloud workload coverage
+ Disaster recovery runbooks & testing
+ Compliance evidence packets

settings.title: Fixed onboarding fee
settings.description: A one-time flat fee sized by environment complexity — not an hourly surprise. You see the number before you sign.

settings.title: 15-minute response SLA
settings.description: Business-hours tickets get a named engineer inside fifteen minutes. Measured monthly and reported transparently in your quarterly review.

settings.title: Documentation you can read
settings.description: A living document of your environment — network, identities, backups, vendors — maintained by us and available to you at any time. Portability is a feature, not a threat.

settings.title: No multi-year lock-in
settings.description: Annual agreements with a thirty-day exit clause. If we aren't earning our retainer, you leave with your documentation and credentials intact.

settings.title: A named team, not a pool
settings.description: You meet the engineers on day one and they stay on the account. Tickets don't bounce to whoever picked up the phone.

settings.quote: We switched because every other quote came in as 'contact us' — Uplink showed us the number on the first call. Six quarters in, we're paying roughly what they said we would.
settings.name: Priya Desai
settings.title: Director of Operations, Holloway & Reed LLP

settings.quote: The Professional tier covered everything our cyber-insurance renewal was asking about. We stopped paying a separate security vendor and our premium actually came down.
settings.name: Daniel Okafor
settings.title: IT Manager, Meridian Professional Services

settings.quote: I wanted published pricing because I was tired of being the office manager stuck on the phone comparing MSP proposals. Uplink made that part of the decision boring, which is exactly what I needed.
settings.name: Rebecca Lindquist
settings.title: Office Manager, Arbor Dental Group
Not sure which tier?
settings.text:
Most clients land in Professional. Essentials fits lean teams under thirty seats; Enterprise fits regulated industries or anyone running their own servers.

A thirty-minute call will tell you which one.

settings.question: What counts as a seat?
settings.answer: An active user with a mailbox or a managed device. Shared kiosks, reception PCs, and meeting-room displays are billed as devices at half a seat. Contractors and occasional access accounts aren't counted unless we're managing their identity.

settings.question: Are servers and cloud workloads extra?
settings.answer: Essentials and Professional cover workstations and SaaS. Servers — physical, virtual, or cloud — are included in Enterprise or added to either lower tier at a flat per-workload rate quoted in the engagement letter.

settings.question: What's in scope vs. a project?
settings.answer: Anything that's part of running the environment you have is in scope. New builds — a cloud migration, office relocation, hardware refresh — are scoped and quoted separately as fixed-fee projects so you know the price going in.

settings.question: Is there a minimum seat count?
settings.answer: Ten seats. Below that, the economics of a dedicated named team don't work — we'll refer you to a partner better suited to very small teams.

settings.question: Can we customise a tier?
settings.answer: Yes, within reason. If you need two Essentials seats and three Enterprise seats inside the same organisation — common for regulated sub-teams — we'll structure that on a single retainer.

settings.text: Get a quote in one call.
Book a free assessment. We'll ask about your seat count, your stack, and your renewals — then tell you which tier fits and what it costs.
